mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits immediately to equity valuations through a lower discount rate and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is already above neutral levels.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is falling versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of rate-decline episodes, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises faster while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightens and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as liquidity conditions worsen.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if combined inflation-plus-tight-policy episodes do not precede negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and consumer demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike episodes are followed by positive 20-day returns more than 45% of the time, or if the signal vanishes after controlling for broader market trend.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth and falling unemployment together indicate broad labor income support for spending, so the S&P 500 should outperform over the next 5 trading days when PAYEMS is rising and UNRATE is not rising.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-strength signal fails to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of observations, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS captures stronger current consumer spending, which should support near-term revenue expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher retail sales do not correspond to positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of rising-sales months, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and slower earnings momentum, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as cyclicals and margins soften.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month industrial declines are followed by positive 60-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal loses significance after accounting for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is accelerating while HOUST is also rising, the economy is expanding through both output and housing demand, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days via stronger earnings breadth.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP-and-housing strength does not produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observations, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either proxy on its own.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate regime, CPIAUCSL cooling while FEDFUNDS remains elevated should ease real-rate pressure and allow equity multiples to expand, creating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL decelerates below its 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-plus-restrictive-policy episodes fail to generate positive 5-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the pattern reverses when inflation cools.
